_comment: WC2026 Round of 32, 2026-06-30, New York/New Jersey Stadium. KNOCKOUT: no draw -- level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES. Routes: France won Group I with a perfect nine points (3-1 Senegal, 3-1 Iraq, 4-1 Norway; GF 10 GA 3, rating 94) -- elite and dominant, one of the tournament favourites. Sweden qualified as a BEST THIRD from Group F (5-1 Tunisia, 1-5 Netherlands, 1-1 Japan; 4 pts, GF 7 GA 7, rating 77) -- an attacking side (7 goals, but the 5 vs minnow Tunisia inflates it) that is also very LEAKY (conceded 7, incl 5 to the Netherlands). Crucially Sweden is NOT an organized low block (unlike Paraguay/Ivory Coast): they are open and leaky, so this is the favourite+goals archetype, not a containment job. Expect France to win, likely WITH goals (Sweden concedes), and a lower ET/penalty risk than the round's tight ties -- the class gap is large and the game should be open. France a heavy favourite by class and form. Home = France (nominal; neutral venue). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. France a heavy favourite (perfect group, elite, one of the tournament favourites) vs an attacking-but-leaky Sweden that qualified as a best third. Favourite+goals archetype: expect France to win with goals, not a tight low-block grind, so a lower shootout risk than the round's other ties.

home.name: France
notes: Elite (rating 94); perfect group, 10 goals, won all three. attack 1.45 -- top-class front line.
Heavy favourite vs an open, leaky side -> expect goals, a comfortable win the modal outcome.

away.name: Sweden
notes: Attacking but the 5-1 vs Tunisia inflates the record; lost 1-5 to the Netherlands. attack 0.85.
LEAKY (conceded 7, not an organized block) -> France should score freely. defense 1.12.
Can score (7 goals) -> BTTS/Over live, but outgunned; lower ET/penalty risk than the tight ties.

_recal: First pass sat 4.4 pts below the market on France (64.4% vs ~69%): France is ELITE (94) and dominant, so don't fade them -- the Spain/Argentina lesson. Lifted FRA 2.00 -> 2.18 and trimmed SWE 0.82 -> 0.78 to align; still favourite+goals.

_lineups._comment: Confirmed/projected XIs (Al Jazeera/RotoWire, ~60 min pre-KO). Two confirmations that keep France the heavy favourite but open the game up: (1) France name a FULL-STRENGTH side despite topping the group on a perfect nine -- Mbappe, Dembele, Olise, Doue all start, no rotation (Saliba questionable but starts; Thuram out, calf). This confirms the no-rotation-discount calibration (Mexico lesson). (2) Sweden start a genuinely dangerous front line -- Isak + Gyokeres + Elanga, two elite strikers -- so they carry real goal threat (BTTS/Over more live than a typical 18% underdog), BUT they are without CB Isak Hien (injured), leaving their already-leaky defence weaker still. Net adjustment: France 2.18 -> 2.22 (Hien out, Sweden's defence weaker) and Sweden 0.78 -> 0.85 (Isak/Gyokeres threat). The SIDE barely moves (France stays ~68%, aligned), but the game projects MORE open/higher-scoring -- a clear favourite that should win with goals rather than grind, and the lowest shootout risk of the round.

_postmatch.result: France 3-0 Sweden (Mbappe brace). France advance comfortably, settled in 90'. The round's clearest favourite delivered without drama.
_postmatch.model_1x2: France 68.0 / Draw 19.6 / Sweden 12.5 (HIGH, 90'); advancement France 80.7 / Sweden 19.3
_postmatch.verdict: Full process hit -- the cleanest of the round. The favourite+goals call was exactly right: France 3-0 is a comfortable win WITH goals and a CLEAN SHEET, not a tight grind. Over 2.5 landed (59.2% read), France's clean sheet landed (42.7%), and Mbappe's brace is the elite star delivering as priced. xG 2.22 -> 3 goals, on the nose. The only half-miss is on the post-XI 'more open' nudge: I lifted both attacks for Sweden's Isak/Gyokeres threat, but France's strong defence (Saliba/Upamecano) shut them out -> BTTS No / clean sheet, not the BTTS Yes the nudge leaned toward. Net: France won by the predicted shape and margin. Crucially this validated the archetype split -- I flagged France as the round's clearest favourite (80.7% advance, lowest shootout risk 8.8%) BECAUSE Sweden is open/leaky, NOT an organized block; that is why it was a comfortable 3-0 rather than the coin-flip-to-penalties shape of the tight low-block ties (Germany-Paraguay, Netherlands-Morocco).
_postmatch.lesson: Classify the underdog FIRST: a large class gap + an OPEN, leaky underdog = favourite+goals (comfortable win, low shootout risk), which is a very different animal from a large gap + an ORGANIZED low block (tight, high ET/penalty risk, real upset equity). France-Sweden is the textbook favourite-advances-clean case that contrasts with the round's penalty upsets. The 'more open' XI nudge was directionally fine on France's goals (Over) but Sweden's dangerous forwards were neutralised by an elite defence -- a reminder that a leaky underdog's attack still has to beat a top back line.
